headlines.title: Iran war live: ‘Positive progress’ as US, Tehran wrap up indirect talks
headlines.snippet: Iran states that a communication channel will be created with Washington to address and discuss alleged violations of the Memorandum of Understanding (MoU). This channel aims to facilitate reporting and discussion on breaches related to the agreement between the two nations.

headlines.title: Couple arrested after daring Empire State marriage proposal stunt
headlines.snippet: Russian daredevils Vanya Beerkus and Angela Nikolau were arrested following a risky marriage proposal stunt at the Empire State Building. They are recognized as ambassadors for 'rooftopping,' a climbing discipline.

headlines.title: US judge sides with NAACP over proposed mail-in ballot restrictions
headlines.snippet: A U.S. judge has ruled in favor of the NAACP regarding proposed restrictions on mail-in ballots. President Trump had advocated for limiting mail-in voting and directed his administration to implement such constraints.

headlines.title: E Jean Carroll demands Donald Trump pay $5.8m in damages from 2019 case
headlines.snippet: E. Jean Carroll's legal team is seeking an expedited resolution for the payment of $5.8 million in damages related to a 2019 case. This request follows the U.S. Supreme Court's decision to decline an appeal.

headlines.title: US signs $1 lease with Israel to build permanent embassy in West Jerusalem
headlines.snippet: The U.S. has finalized a $1 lease agreement with Israel to construct a permanent embassy in West Jerusalem. This deal allocates the necessary land for the establishment of the diplomatic facility.

headlines.title: Qatari official says 'positive progress' made on US-Iran MoU
headlines.snippet: A Qatari official reported significant progress in discussions concerning a U.S.-Iran Memorandum of Understanding. Talks were held between Qatari and Pakistani mediators, alongside U.S. and Iranian delegations in Doha.

headlines.title: Airlines restore some Middle East flights as suspensions continue
headlines.snippet: Some airlines are resuming flights to the Middle East as diplomatic efforts to resolve the conflict with Iran intensify. However, many carriers maintain existing flight suspensions. Air Canada, Air France, KLM, and Delta have announced cancellations or suspensions to various destinations.

headlines.title: Hormuz shipping may take months to return to normal, French shipping company says
headlines.snippet: The French shipping company CMA CGM anticipates that the shipping situation in the Strait of Hormuz may take several months to normalize. The company has experienced vessels being held within the Gulf since the conflict began.

headlines.title: Oil prices fall as US leaders strike positive note on Iran talks
headlines.snippet: Oil prices experienced a decline of over 1 percent following positive statements from U.S. leaders regarding talks with Tehran. Brent and West Texas Intermediate crude benchmarks reached their lowest levels since March.

headlines.title: Iranian parliament speaker says IAEA barred from bombed nuclear sites
headlines.snippet: The Iranian parliament speaker stated that the IAEA is not permitted to inspect nuclear sites that have been bombed or damaged. Access has been granted to only two sites, including the Bushehr power plant.

headlines.title: Trump says Spain ‘Not a Good NATO Member’
headlines.snippet: Trump stated that Spain is not a good member of NATO.

headlines.title: Iran says to use frozen funds in Qatar to buy ‘required goods’
headlines.snippet: Iran has announced its intention to utilize frozen funds held in Qatar to procure necessary goods.

headlines.title: Russian strikes in Ukraine kill six, wound dozens
headlines.snippet: Russian strikes in Ukraine resulted in six fatalities and dozens of injuries. The attacks occurred amidst ongoing conflict in the region.

headlines.title: Lebanon, Syria join CENTCOM-led Middle East security dialogue for first time
headlines.snippet: Lebanon and Syria are participating in a security dialogue for the Middle East, led by CENTCOM, for the first time. This marks a significant step in regional security cooperation.

headlines.title: Giant Container Ship ‘Notre-Dame’ Arrives in France
headlines.snippet: The large container ship 'Notre-Dame' has arrived in France, marking the completion of its journey. The ship's arrival is a notable event in maritime transport.

headlines.title: World Bank approves Morocco clean energy project
headlines.snippet: The World Bank has approved $265 million in financing to support the construction of a pumped hydropower storage plant in Morocco. This project aims to enhance renewable energy infrastructure.

headlines.title: Trump sees progress as US, Iran hold Qatar talks
headlines.snippet: Trump indicated that indirect talks with Iran in Doha were progressing, suggesting a continued diplomatic path despite recent escalations.

headlines.title: Attackers target homes of Greek ruling party figures, killing one
headlines.snippet: Attackers used homemade explosives to target the homes of three Greek ruling party politicians on Wednesday, resulting in one fatality. The incidents have raised security concerns.

headlines.title: Turkey, Iraq discuss cooperation ahead of pipeline deal expiry
headlines.snippet: Turkey and Iraq are discussing cooperation regarding the Iraq-Turkey Crude Oil Pipeline ahead of its deal expiry. This discussion is crucial for energy transport between the two nations.

headlines.title: Body tasked with Gaza governance says Cyprus talks 'highly productive'
headlines.snippet: The committee responsible for Gaza's governance described its recent talks in Cyprus as highly productive. The discussions focused on alleviating suffering and facilitating reconstruction in the region.

headlines.title: مصادر للميادين: مكتب الأمم المتحدة في مقديشو تسلم منظومات اتصالات إسرائيلية متطورة
headlines.snippet: Reports indicate that the United Nations office in Mogadishu received advanced Israeli communication systems. The details surrounding this delivery remain under scrutiny.

headlines.title: بين تشييع السيد خامنئي والمفاوضات... كيف تُرسم معادلات ما بعد الحرب؟
headlines.snippet: The article discusses the political landscape and negotiations following the passing of the former Iranian Supreme Leader, exploring the potential shifts in regional dynamics.

headlines.title: وزير الخارجية السعودي الأمير فيصل بن فرحان في زيارة لبكين... ما التفاصيل؟
headlines.snippet: Saudi Arabia's Foreign Minister, Prince Faisal bin Farhan, is undertaking a visit to Beijing. The purpose of the visit involves discussions on bilateral relations and regional issues.

headlines.title: كاميرا الميادين ترافق الجمهور المكسيكي أثناء احتفاله بتأهل المنتخب للدور القادم
headlines.snippet: This report follows Mexican supporters celebrating their national team's advancement to the next round of the competition. Their enthusiasm was captured by Al Mayadeen's camera.

headlines.title: "الخيط القرمزي" جدار فصل جديد ينشأه الاحتلال الإسرائيلي شرقي الضفة
headlines.snippet: A new separation barrier, referred to as 'The Crimson Thread,' is being erected by Israeli forces east of the West Bank. This barrier is a significant development in the region.

headlines.title: Iran prevents transit of over 3,600 kilograms of methamphetamine to Europe
headlines.snippet: Iran has announced the successful interception of over 3,600 kilograms of methamphetamine intended for Europe. The official highlighted the significant cost Iran incurs for global security while facing sanctions.

headlines.title: Iran warns UN of Israel’s ‘deliberate’ and ‘public’ threat against Leader of Islamic Revolution
headlines.snippet: Iran's UN Ambassador Amir Saeid Iravani has warned the UN and Security Council members about a direct threat from Israel against the Leader of the Islamic Revolution. He emphasized the seriousness of the threat.

headlines.title: Pezeshkian: Government would have fully complied if Leader had ordered not to negotiate
headlines.snippet: President Masoud Pezeshkian stated that the government would have fully complied if the Leader of the Islamic Revolution had directed them not to negotiate. This underscores adherence to the Supreme Leader's guidance.

headlines.title: Gharibabadi: Doha talks focused on US violations of MoU by US, frozen asstes
headlines.snippet: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister for Legal and International Affairs indicated that talks in Doha focused on U.S. violations of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding. A working group will monitor the agreement's implementation.

headlines.title: Armed forces on high alert as Iran prepares for Ayatollah Khamenei’s funeral
headlines.snippet: Iran's armed forces have increased their presence along the country's borders to ensure security during the funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei. This measure aims to maintain stability.

headlines.title: Talks btw Iran, US for a final agreement not yet started
headlines.snippet: Talks between Iran and the U.S. concerning a final agreement have not yet commenced.

headlines.title: Any threat against Iranians to receive immediate response
headlines.snippet: Iran has stated that any threat directed towards its citizens will be met with an immediate response.

headlines.title: Iran’s missile, UAV capabilities nonnegotiable: Defense min.
headlines.snippet: Iran's Defense Minister declared that the country's missile and UAV capabilities are non-negotiable.

headlines.title: Israeli forces blow up homes in three south Lebanon villages
headlines.snippet: Israeli forces have destroyed homes in three villages located in southern Lebanon.

headlines.title: Iran presents proposals for new SCO bank
headlines.snippet: Iran has put forth proposals for the establishment of a new bank within the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO).

headlines.title: Iran parliament cries censorship after Ghalibaf interview cut short
headlines.snippet: The Iranian parliament has voiced concerns about censorship following the premature termination of an interview with Ghalibaf.

headlines.title: Iranians barely visible in US-Iran deal, UN rapporteur says - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: A UN rapporteur has observed that Iranians have a minimal presence in the U.S.-Iran deal.

headlines.snippet: The IRGC is reportedly taking steps to seize a historic Protestant church located in Tehran.

headlines.snippet: Ghalibaf has provided details regarding $12 billion in Iranian assets accessible through the central bank.

headlines.snippet: Approximately 100,000 individuals have been killed in Myanmar since the post-coup conflict began, according to a monitoring group.

headlines.title: Australia boosts shark-spotting drone coverage at Sydney beaches
headlines.snippet: Australia is enhancing its shark-spotting drone coverage at Sydney's beaches. This initiative aims to improve beach safety for swimmers.

headlines.title: Industrial sector grows 4.8% in Q1 2026, drives 40% of economic growth — JCI
headlines.snippet: The industrial sector experienced a growth of 4.8% in the first quarter of 2026, contributing significantly to the overall economic expansion.

headlines.title: Israeli settlement expansion ‘tool of colonial cleansing, elimination’ — Royal committee
headlines.snippet: The expansion of Israeli settlements is described as a 'tool of colonial cleansing and elimination' by a royal committee.

headlines.title: Syria president names last members to finalise first post-Assad parliament
headlines.snippet: The Syrian president has appointed the final members of the first post-Assad parliament. This completes the formation of the new legislative body.

headlines.title: Salam: We will not yield to blackmail or threats of civil war
headlines.snippet: Salam stated that they will not yield to blackmail or threats of civil war, emphasizing a firm stance against destabilizing pressures.

headlines.title: Lebanon announces in Paris its intention to abolish the death penalty
headlines.snippet: Lebanon has announced its intention in Paris to abolish the death penalty. This represents a significant step towards judicial reform.

headlines.title: US signs agreement to build permanent embassy in Jerusalem
headlines.snippet: The U.S. has signed an agreement to construct a permanent embassy in Jerusalem.

headlines.title: House of Representatives rejects resolution calling for 'withdrawal of US armed forces from all hostilities in Lebanon'
headlines.snippet: The House of Representatives has rejected a resolution that called for the withdrawal of U.S. armed forces from all hostilities in Lebanon.

headlines.title: Indirect Iran-US technical talks underway in Doha, source says
headlines.snippet: Indirect technical talks between Iran and the U.S. are currently taking place in Doha, according to a source.

headlines.title: White House lifts export control on Anthropic that froze its most advanced models
headlines.snippet: The U.S. government has lifted export controls on Anthropic's most advanced AI models, specifically Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5. Access to these models will be gradually restored.

headlines.title: Oman has delivered a proposal on the future of the Strait of Hormuz to the US, sources say
headlines.snippet: Oman has presented a proposal to the U.S. and its allies regarding the future of the Strait of Hormuz. The proposal includes plans for shipping companies to pay service fees for passage.

headlines.title: The Strait of Hormuz pain has been China’s gain, The Asia Group says
headlines.snippet: China has benefited significantly from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, according to The Asia Group. The disruption of oil and commodity flow has impacted Asian economies unevenly.

headlines.title: US, Iranian officials hold indirect, lower-level talks in Doha, source says
headlines.snippet: U.S. and Iranian officials are engaged in indirect, lower-level technical discussions in Doha, facilitated by Qatari and Pakistani mediators. Special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner met with Qatar's prime minister.

headlines.title: Egypt’s Former Irrigation Minister warns not to underestimate impact of GERD
headlines.snippet: Former Egyptian Minister of Water Resources and Irrigation Mohamed Nasr-Eddin Allam cautioned against underestimating the impact of the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD) on Egypt and Sudan.

headlines.snippet: Israeli forces reportedly assaulted mourners at a funeral in Jerusalem. The incident occurred during a period of heightened tensions.

headlines.snippet: The occupation authorities have issued notices for the demolition of three residential structures south of Hebron.
